steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Set up the quadratic equation
steps.content:
The area of a rectangle equals width times length. With width $n$ and length $n+5$:
$$n(n+5) = 204$$
Expand and rearrange to standard form:
$$n^2 + 5n - 204 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor and solve
steps.content:
To factor $n^2 + 5n - 204 = 0$, we need two numbers that multiply to $-204$ and add to $5$.

Factor pairs of 204: we know $204 = 12 \times 17$. If we use the pair $(17, -12)$: we get $17 \times (-12) = -204$ and $17 + (-12) = 5$.
$$(n+17)(n-12) = 0$$
Solutions: $n = -17$ or $n = 12$. Since $n$ represents the width of a rectangle, it must be positive. Therefore, $n = 12$.
